title: Bayes' Theorem Application
mermaid: graph TD A1[Define Prior Probability P of A] --> B1[Input: P of A] A2[Define Conditional Probability P of B given A] --> B2[Input: P of B given A] A3[Define Marginal Probability P of B] --> B3[Input: P of B or P of B given not A and P of not A] B1 --> C1{Is P of B directly available?} B2 --> C1 B3 --> C1 C1 -->|Yes| D1[Calculate P of A times P of B given A] C1 -->|No| E1[Calculate P of not A equals 1 minus P of A] E1 --> F1[Calculate P of B equals P of B given A times P of A plus P of B given not A times P of not A] D1 --> G1[Intermediate: Numerator equals P of A times P of B given A] F1 --> G2[Intermediate: Denominator equals P of B] D1 --> H1[Calculate P of A given B equals P of B given A times P of A divided by P of B] G1 --> H1 G2 --> H1 H1 --> I1[Output: Posterior Probability P of A given B] style A1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff style A2 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff style A3 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff style B1 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000 style B2 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000 style B3 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000 style C1 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000 style D1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff style E1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff style F1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff style H1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff style G1 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff style G2 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff style I1 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
entities: Define Prior Probability P of A
Define Conditional Probability P of B given A
Input: P of B given A
Define Marginal Probability P of B
Input: P of B or P of B given not A and P of not A
Is P of B directly available?} B2 --> C1 B3 --> C1 C1 -->|Yes| D1[Calculate P of A times P of B given A
Calculate P of not A equals 1 minus P of A
Calculate P of B equals P of B given A times P of A plus P of B given not A times P of not A
Intermediate: Numerator equals P of A times P of B given A
Intermediate: Denominator equals P of B
Calculate P of A given B equals P of B given A times P of A divided by P of B
Output: Posterior Probability P of A given B
